note: Tuned CVRP solver settings accepted in Stage 10-A. Budgets/timeouts still come from final_experiment_plan.json; these are per-algorithm parameter overrides validated on all six official instances with seeds 42-44.

alns.validation_note: Improves P-n16-k8 (-0.14), A-n80-k10 (-1.01) and X-n101-k25 (-2.07) best gaps, ties E/A32, but regresses M-n200-k17 by +1.02. Accepted for the net mean improvement; the solver default stays enhanced_operators=false so base behavior is unchanged without this config.

alns_advanced.validation_note: Stage 11-B intensification (relocate + swap + Or-opt + 2-opt*) applied to both ALNS variants when a new best is found and as a final polish. Seeds 42-44 on scaled budgets: A-n80-k10 4.92 -> 4.66, X-n101-k25 26.79 -> 26.68, M-n200-k17 8.41 -> 7.98 mean gap, all feasible. P-n16-k8 regressed 0.30 -> 0.39, so the pass only activates for instances with at least min_customers customers (a size threshold like the bnb_lds small-instance mode, not an instance name list). candidate_list_k 10/20/None produced identical solutions; k=10 is the fastest.

alns_policy.main_result_rule: Both ALNS variants run in the final rerun (rows cvrp_alns and cvrp_alns_enhanced). The assignment 'alns' result uses cvrp_alns_enhanced for every instance except M-n200-k17, where Stage 10-A validation showed a +1.02% regression; M-n200-k17 uses the basic cvrp_alns row. This rule was declared before the rerun, not after it.

ga_island.advanced_note: Stage 11-B memetic step: polish the best solution every local_search_every generations and reinject its chromosome. Seeds 42-44 on scaled budgets: A-n80-k10 4.95 -> 4.66 (no longer baseline-stuck), X-n101-k25 24.87 -> 24.84, M-n200-k17 8.45 -> 8.01 mean gap. P-n16-k8 regressed slightly, so the same size gate as alns_advanced applies (advanced_min_customers).

bnb_lds.note: Stage 10-F small-instance mode: instances with at most 25 customers get a much deeper discrepancy budget (still timeout-capped, still falls back to the incumbent). Measured effect: P-n16-k8 2.65% -> 0.30% and E-n22-k4 3.34% -> 0.07%, both in about a second. A-n32-k5 stays at baseline even at depth 12 (tested and rejected there), and larger instances keep the original settings. The threshold is by customer count, not instance names.
